segments.s552-g57: همی is a poetic/emphatic particle placed before the verb phrase (here meaning roughly 'thus' or 'just/indeed'). تازه شد is a compound of the adjective تازه 'new' with the past of شدن 'to become', so 'became new' / 'was renewed'. رسم شاه اردشیر is the subject phrase ('the custom/rite of King Ardashir') placed after the predicate in inverted poetic word order.
segments.s552-g58: بدو is a prepositional/ directional clitic meaning 'to him' or 'for him', showing the joy is directed at Ardashir. شاد گشتند is 'became happy' with گشتند (past plural of گشتن) agreeing with the plural subject; برنا و پیر ('young and old') is the coordinated subject following the predicate in poetic inversion.
segments.s552-g9: همه گوش دارید is an address to the audience: همه ('everyone') + گوش دارید literally 'have ear' — an idiom meaning 'pay attention' or 'listen'. فرمان من follows as the object 'my command' (فرمان followed by the genitive من). داريد is present indicative (2nd-person plural) used to call for attention.
segments.s552-g10: مگردید is a negative imperative/present admonition 'do not turn away / do not break'. یکسر here is an adverb meaning 'entirely/at all' modifying the verb. ز (poetic for از) introduces the object of prohibition, 'from my پیمان' = 'my covenant', with من marking possession.
segments.s552-g11: وزین is an adjective meaning 'weighty' or 'grave' describing the following utterances. هرچ گویم literally 'whatever I say' — هرچ (whatever) + گویم (1st-person present subjunctive/indicative 'I say' in classical style). پژوهش کنید is a 2nd-person plural imperative 'investigate/examine' (پژوهش as noun used as verbal object, کنید 'do').
segments.s552-g12: وگر is a conditional 'but if'. خام گویم = 'if I say (something) raw/unripe/naive' (خام describing گویم). نکوهش کنید is again a 2nd-person plural imperative: نکوهش 'reproach, blame' used as the object and کنید 'do' — 'blame (me)'. The sentence sets a courteous conditional: praise investigation of serious words, reproach small or foolish ones.
segments.s552-g13: چو من دیدم is a temporal clause 'when I saw' (چو = 'when', من = subject, دیدم = past 'I saw'). اکنون 'now' sets the time. به سود و زیان is a prepositional phrase 'regarding/to profit and loss' (به + noun phrase), pointing to the domain of what is seen.
segments.s552-g14: دو بخشش نهاده شد اندر میان is the main clause: دو بخشش 'two divisions/portions' (بخشش used here in the sense 'a part / division' — archaic/poetic use), نهاده شد is a passive/perfect construction 'were placed' or 'had been set', and اندر میان 'in between' / 'among' indicates location. The whole means two parts were established between profit and loss.
segments.s552-g15: یکی begins an enumeration: 'one (of them)' or 'one is...' — here functioning as 'a certain' or 'one (of the two)'. پادشا (variant of پادشاه) 'king' is predicated, and پاسبان جهان 'guardian/protector of the world' is an appositive/title: noun + modifier (پاسبان as role, جهان the domain). Word order is noun phrases stacked as epithets.
segments.s552-g16: نگهبان گنج کهان و مهان is a continuation of titles: نگهبان 'guardian' taking گنج کهان 'the treasure(s) of the universe/ages' as its object (کهان = the world/ages) and و مهان 'and of the guests' (مهان here listed as another domain). The sequence lists spheres the king guards; the nouns follow a paratactic, poetic style.
segments.s552-g17: وگر introduces a conditional ('if') and the clause follows subject–circumstance order: شاه (subject) + با داد و فرخ (prepositional phrase 'with justice and prosperity'). پیست is an archaic/poetic 3rd-person copular/verbal form meaning 'is' or 'it follows' and closes the conditional clause.
segments.s552-g18: خرد is the subject placed at the start for emphasis; بی‌گمان is an adverb ('undoubtedly'). پاسبان is a predicate noun ('guardian'), and ویست (poetic form 'وی است') is the copula 'is' (3rd person), here carrying a pronominal sense of 'his/for him' in this construction.
segments.s552-g19: Here باشد is used optatively/subjunctively ('may be' / 'let be') rather than a simple declarative 'is'. خرد (subject) is predicated as پاسبان ('guardian'), and و links an additional predicative noun نیک‌خواه ('well-wisher' / 'benevolent one').
segments.s552-g20: سرش is سر + enclitic ش ('his head'); برگذارد is a prefixed verb (بر + گذاردن) used here in a hortative/subjunctive sense 'may (he) set/lift/put aside'. ز is the poetic form of 'from', and ابر سیاه ('black/dark cloud') is a metaphorical noun phrase. Word order places the possessed noun before the verb, as common in Persian.
segments.s552-g21: همه here functions adverbially ('entirely/wholly'); جستنش is a noun/gerund phrase ('his seeking' or 'the act of seeking it') and is object of داد 'gave', where داد = 'gave/paid (in effort)' rather than 'justice'. The clause closes with و + دانش بود where دانش is predicative ('it was knowledge') and بود is the past copula.
segments.s552-g22: ز marks cause or source ('from/because of'); دانش is the source ('knowledge'); روانش is روان + possessive ش ('his soul/spirit'); به رامش بود uses به + noun to express the resulting state ('was in tranquility'), with بود as the copula in the past sense.
segments.s552-g23: دگر here introduces a contrastive 'another / on the other hand' sense; آنک is a relative/conjunction ('that/when') linking the subordinate clause; او is the subject 'he'; بآزمون is an instrumental phrase 'by/with trial' (testing); and خرد is the object 'wisdom' being tested.
segments.s552-g24: بکوشد is 3rd-person present/subjunctive of کوشیدن ('he strives/attempts'); بمردی is an adverbial phrase 'in manhood / manfully' modifying the verb; و links the next verb phrase; گرد آورد is a compound verbal idea (گرد + آورد) meaning 'to gather/bring together' — گرد here participates in a verbal compound and آور/آورد is 'bring'.
segments.s552-g49: بهر سو (prepositional phrase) = 'toward every direction' placed before the verb for emphasis; فرستیم is the 1st‑person plural form used here in a hortative/jussive sense ('let us send'), and کارآگهان is a plural noun 'the informed/knowledgeable ones' serving as the (unsuffixed) object of فرستیم.
segments.s552-g50: بجوییم is another 1st‑person plural hortative/subjunctive 'let us seek/search'; بیدار functions adverbially ('alertly' or 'vigilantly') modifying بجوییم; کار جهان is a noun + noun genitive ('the affairs of the world') and is the object of بجوییم (the ezāfe linking is omitted in poetic orthography).
segments.s552-g51: نخواهیم is a negative 1st‑person plural ('we will not want' / 'we do not desire'); هرگز 'ever/never' intensifies the negation; به جز is the compound prepositional phrase 'except'; آفرین here is the noun 'praise/acclamation' (not the verbal stem).
segments.s552-g52: که introduces a relative clause 'which/that'; بر ما کنند literally 'they do/make upon us' = 'they bestow on us' with کنند in the present/optative sense; از جهان‌آفرین specifies source/authority 'from the Creator of the World' (i.e. bestowed by the world's Creator).
segments.s552-g53: مهان و کهان پاک is a coordinated subject: 'the guests and the (people of the) world who are pure' (پاک is an adjective applied after the coordination); برخاستند is the simple past 'they rose/stood up.' Word order places the compound subject before the verb, as typical.
segments.s552-g54: زبان را uses را as the definite/accusative marker ('the tongue' = 'speech') as direct object; به خوبی is an adverbial prepositional phrase 'with goodness/beautifully'; بیاراستند is the past plural 'they adorned/embellished' (بی‑ + آراستن).
segments.s552-g55: به شاپور is the dative 'to Shapur'; بر آفرین is the phrase 'the acclamation upon/for him' (بر here marks the target of the shout); خواندند is past plural 'they uttered/recited' (they gave the acclamation).
segments.s552-g56: زبرجد is the object 'peridots/gems'; به تاجش 'onto his crown' with تاجش = تاج + -ش ('his crown'); برافشاندند is the past plural 'they scattered/sprinkled/showered' with the prefix بـــ indicating motion onto/over the crown.
segments.s552-g33: به + noun phrase here marks direction/goal: 'toward' or 'to'. 'آسایش و نیک‌نامی' is a coordinated noun phrase (comfort/peace and good repute). 'گرای' is an imperative/verb-stem meaning 'turn (yourself) toward' or 'orient' and links as the main verb commanding orientation toward the preceding objects.
segments.s552-g34: This is an independent imperative clause: 'گریزان شو' = 'become avoiding / shun' (imperative 'شو' with participial/active adjective 'گریزان'). 'از' introduces the object of avoidance ('from / of'). 'مرد ناپاک رای' is a genitive-like noun phrase 'a man of impure/corrupt counsel' (ناپاک qualifies مرد, رای = counsel/opinion).
segments.s552-g35: 'به چیز کسان دست یازد کسی' literally puts 'به' + 'چیز کسان' (others' things/possessions) as the goal of the action. 'دست یازد' is a verbal phrase meaning 'lays hands on / seizes' (دست + verb یاز/یازد), and 'کسی' is the indefinite subject 'someone/anyone'. Word order is object/goal first, then the verb and indefinite subject.
segments.s552-g36: This relative clause introduced by 'که' modifies 'کسی': 'who/that does not have sufficient فرهنگ (refinement/learning) for him'. 'بهرش' = 'for him/to him' (the dative/benefactive), 'نباشد' is a negative present/subjunctive copula ('is not / does not have'), and 'بسی' = 'enough/sufficient'. The clause gives the condition under which someone should not seize others' things.
segments.s552-g37: 'مرا' (me / my) appears with the object marker 'را' placed after it; here it functions as the possessor in a concessive/emphatic construction meaning 'my (affection) toward you'. 'بر شما' = 'toward you'. 'زان' = 'from that / than that' introduces the comparison, 'فزونست' is a present copular/verb meaning 'is greater / exceeds', and 'مهر' = 'affection / love' is the predicate noun. Word order is roughly 'my — toward you — from that — greater is — affection' (poetic inversion).
segments.s552-g38: 'که' introduces a relative/comparative clause 'that which'. 'اختر' = 'star' is the subject of 'نماید' (the verb 'to show/display'); 'همی' is an adverb meaning 'ever/continually', and 'بر سپهر' = 'upon the sky/heavens' (بر as locative 'on/upon'). The clause means 'that which a star continually displays on the sky', completing the comparison.
segments.s552-g39: 'همان رسم' = 'the same custom/rule'; 'شاه بلند اردشیر' is a nominal group with the epithet 'بلند' (lofty/high) modifying 'شاه' and 'اردشیر' as the name in apposition: 'the lofty king Ardashir'. The whole segment names the custom to be performed.
segments.s552-g40: 'بجای آورم' is a verbal phrase 'I bring about / I perform' with بجای = 'in place of / in the stead' used idiomatically as 'to carry out, to fulfill'. 'با شما' = 'for/with you' (here 'for you' — i.e., on your behalf or among you). 'ناگزیر' is an adjective/adverb meaning 'compelled / necessarily' and here indicates that the speaker is obliged to perform the action.
segments.s552-g25: Poetic word order: به دانش ز یزدان شناسد سپاس reads literally 'by knowledge, from God, recognizes praise.' به دانش (by/through knowledge) marks the means; ز یزدان ('from God' or 'of God') qualifies the source of that knowledge; شناسد is a 3rd-person present/epic verb 'he/one recognizes' (poetic -د ending); سپاس is the direct object 'praise/what is to be praised.' The clause states that through God-given knowledge one comes to know what merits praise.
segments.s552-g26: This is a nominal clause describing a person: خنک مرد دانا و یزدان‌شناس. خنک is an attributive adjective ('cool/pleasant/calm') placed before مرد ('man/person'); دانا ('wise') further qualifies him; و links to یزدان‌شناس ('one who knows God' i.e. pious/God-knower). The adjectives follow Persian adjective order (adjective + noun), and the whole phrase picks up as the comment about the praiseworthy person.
segments.s552-g27: به شاهی خردمند باشد سزا is a statement of propriety: به شاهی ('to/for the kingship') indicates the beneficiary or domain; خردمند ('a wise [one]') is the logical possessor; باشد is a classical present/subjunctive copula meaning 'should be / is fitting to be'; سزا here means 'desert/that which is fitting' (i.e. 'it is fitting that...'). Together: 'It is fitting that kingship belong to the wise.'
segments.s552-g28: به جای خرد زر شود بی‌بها places two nouns in contrast: به جای خرد ('in place of/instead of wisdom') sets up the comparison; زر ('gold') is the subject that follows; شود is the 3rd-person present 'becomes'; بی‌بها is a descriptive predicate meaning 'without price / of no value' in this context. The intended meaning is that gold is of no worth when compared to wisdom.
segments.s552-g29: توانگر شود هرک خشنود گشت is a conditional/general statement with inverted order: هرک ('whoever') is the relative subject; خشنود گشت ('becomes pleased/content') is the predicate for that person; توانگر شود ('becomes wealthy') is the result clause (become + adjective → 'become rich'). The verbs گشت and شود are classical perfective/present-style forms meaning 'becomes', so the line reads 'Whoever becomes content becomes wealthy.'
segments.s552-g30: دل آرزو خانه دود گشت uses compact poetic word order: دل آرزو ('the heart of desire' = 'the heart given to longing') is the subject phrase; خانه دود گشت ('the house became smoke') is the verbal clause with دود as predicate nominal and گشت 'became' as verb. The line pictures the destructive effect of desire—when the heart is full of craving, the household is reduced to smoke.
segments.s552-g31: کرا آرزو بیش تیمار بیش is an aphoristic relative clause: کرا ('whoever') introduces the generalizer; آرزو بیش ('more/too much desire') and تیمار بیش ('more/greater care/worry') are parallel noun phrases indicating cause and effect. The construction asserts that greater desire brings greater solicitude/worry.
segments.s552-g32: This is a set of imperatives/advice: بکوش is an imperative 'strive/endeavor'; ونیوش is a contracted poetic form (و + نوش) meaning roughly 'and taste/drink/enjoy' (an imperative jussive), and منه آز پیش is an admonition 'do not let greed come before you'—منه here functions as an archaic negative imperative ('do not take/let'), followed by آز ('greed') and پیش ('before/in front'). Altogether: 'Strive and enjoy, but do not let greed be foremost.'
segments.s552-g1: چو is the subordinating conjunction 'when'. شاپور is the 3rd‑person referent (the king's name). بنشست is a simple past 3rd‑person singular verb 'sat down'. بر تخت داد is a prepositional phrase: بر + تخت (throne) with داد ('justice') qualifying تخت to mean 'throne of justice' — a noun‑noun compound acting as the place of sitting.
segments.s552-g2: کلاه دلفروز is a noun phrase 'the Dalfrooz cap/crown' where دلفروز functions as a name or epithet modifying کلاه. بر سر is the usual prepositional phrase 'on (the) head'. نهاد is the past of نهادن 'to place/put' (3rd‑person singular), so the whole clause means 'he placed the Dalfrooz cap on (his) head.'
segments.s552-g3: شدند is the past plural 'they became/there were formed' (here used impersonally: 'an assembly was formed'). انجمن is the noun 'assembly/meeting'. پیش او is a locative/prepositional phrase 'in front of him / in his presence'. بخردان (literally 'the wise') specifies who composed that assembly; in classical/poetic Persian the linking ezāfe is often omitted, so انجمنِ بخردان = 'assembly of the wise.'
segments.s552-g4: This is a noun phrase listing those present: بزرگان 'great ones/elders' modified by فرزانه 'wise/learned', coordinated with و 'and' followed by موبدان 'mōbeds' (Zoroastrian priests). Adjectives and titles appear in apposition; word order places the head noun before modifiers or coordinating items.
segments.s552-g5: چنین گفت is a fixed reporting phrase 'thus said' (چنین = 'thus'; گفت here introduces speech). کای is an archaic/vocative particle 'O' used to address a person or assembly. نامدار انجمن is the vocative phrase 'famed assembly' (نامدار qualifying انجمن).
segments.s552-g6: This continues the address: بزرگان 'great ones' + پردانش 'full of knowledge/learned' and و 'and' رای‌زن 'counsel‑givers/advisors'. پردانش uses the prefix پر- ('full of'), and رای‌زن is a compound meaning 'one who gives counsel.' Together they form a coordinated apposition describing the assembly.
segments.s552-g7: منم is the emphatic present 'I am' (colloquial/informal contracted form of 'من هستم'). پاک here functions as a name or epithet following the copula; the phrase فرزند شاه اردشیر is a genitive chain 'son of King Ardashir' (classical Persian often omits the linking ezāfe in such sequences). The whole segment is a self‑identification.
segments.s552-g8: سراینده is a participial/noun form 'composer/poet/author' used predicatively to describe the speaker. دانش 'knowledge/learning' is its object/field ('composer of learning'), and و یادگیر 'and learner' gives a parallel role — the speaker calls himself both a maker/composer of learning and one who learns. Coordination links the two roles.
segments.s552-g41: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' (modern از). The clause has V‑final word order: subject/phrase 'ز دهقان' (from the peasants) plus the first‑person negative verb نخواهم ('I will not want / I will not demand'). جز ... از ... یکی is a restrictive construction meaning 'except one out of ...' (جز = 'except'; از سی یکی = 'one of thirty').
segments.s552-g42: درم is the small coin (dirham) being counted; تا here introduces purpose/result ('so that / in order that') and links to the verb دهم later in the clause ('that I may give'). به + لشکر marks the indirect object 'to/for the army', and اندکی is an adverbial noun meaning 'a little / a small amount.' The verb دهم('I give') is the intended action governed by تا.
segments.s552-g43: The construction 'مرا ... هست' is the idiomatic Persian existential/possessive pattern meaning 'I have ...' (literally 'to me ... is'). The nouns (خوبی و گنج آباد) precede the copula هست; گنج آباد is a compound where آباد qualifies گنج ('a well‑stocked / prosperous treasure'). را after مرا is the object/definite marker emphasizing possession.
segments.s552-g44: A simple nominal list followed by the copula هست: the nouns دلیری و مردی و بنیاد are coordinated ('bravery and manliness and foundation') and هست asserts their existence or presence. مردی here is best read as the abstract quality 'manliness / chivalry' not a single man.
segments.s552-g45: ز چیز کسان = 'of/from people's things/possessions' (ز = 'from/of', چیز = 'thing/possession', کسان = 'people'). بی‌نیازیم is first‑person plural present 'we are free of need / self‑sufficient' and نیز adds 'also / too.' The order places the source phrase before the predicate.
segments.s552-g46: که begins a causal subordinate clause here ('because / for'), explaining the reason: دشمن شود مردم = 'people become enemies' (شود = 'become/happen'). از بهر چیز = 'over/for the sake of a thing' (از بهر = 'because of / over'), i.e. people quarrel and become enemies on account of possessions.
segments.s552-g47: This is an inverted clause: راه (the way/path) is the semantic subject and گشتاده‌ست (poetic/archaic form akin to گشاده‌ست) means 'has been opened' or 'is made wide.' بر ما indicates the direction/beneficiary 'toward us' and شما را marks 'for you' (object/emphatic). Literally: 'Toward us, a path for you has been opened.'
segments.s552-g48: به مهریم = 'by/with my kindness/favor' (به + مهرِ‑ـیم), where مهرِـیم expresses goodwill; با مردم نیک‌خواه means 'among/with benevolent, well‑wishing people.' This phrase qualifies the manner or social context in which the path has been opened.

sentences.s552-s29: Thus the custom of King Ardashir was renewed; young and old became joyful for him.
sentences.s552-s5: All of you, listen to my command; do not at all break my covenant.
sentences.s552-s6: And whatever weighty thing I say, examine it; but if I say something crude, blame me.
sentences.s552-s7: When I saw that now, with regard to profit and loss, two portions had been placed between (them),
sentences.s552-s8: One is a king, a guardian of the world, a keeper of the treasures of the ages and of the guests.
sentences.s552-s9: And if the king is endowed with justice and good fortune, wisdom will without doubt be his guardian.
sentences.s552-s10: May wisdom be the guardian, and may the well-wisher lift his head out of the black cloud.
sentences.s552-s11: He devoted himself entirely to seeking, and that was knowledge; from knowledge his soul had tranquility.
sentences.s552-s12: But when he tests his wisdom, he will strive like a man and gather (it).
sentences.s552-s25: Let us send informed men in every direction, and diligently seek the affairs of the world.
sentences.s552-s26: We will want nothing ever except praise—that which people bestow upon us, from the Creator of the world.
sentences.s552-s27: The guests and the pure of the world rose up; they adorned their speech with goodness.
sentences.s552-s28: They cried 'Bravo!' to Shapur; they showered peridots upon his crown.
sentences.s552-s17: Turn toward ease and a good reputation; shun a man of corrupt counsel.
sentences.s552-s18: Do not lay hands on other people's belongings — one who lacks sufficient refinement should not.
sentences.s552-s19: My affection for you is greater than that which a star continually shows upon the heavens.
sentences.s552-s20: I must perform for you the same custom of the lofty king Ardashir.
sentences.s552-s13: By knowledge of God one recognizes what is praiseworthy; calm is the man who is wise and who knows God.
sentences.s552-s14: Kingship ought to be for the wise; in the place of wisdom, gold becomes of no value.
sentences.s552-s15: Whoever becomes content becomes wealthy; when the heart is given to desire, the house turns to smoke.
sentences.s552-s16: Whoever has excessive desire has excessive care; strive and enjoy, and do not let greed come before you.
sentences.s552-s1: When Shapur sat upon the throne of justice, he placed the Dalfrooz crown on his head.
sentences.s552-s2: An assembly of the wise was formed before him — great, learned elders and mobeds.
sentences.s552-s3: Thus he said, 'O famed assembly — great, learned men and counselors...'
sentences.s552-s4: I am Manam-Pak, son of King Ardashir — a composer of learning and a learner.
sentences.s552-s21: From the peasants I will take nothing but one out of thirty dirhams, so that I may give a little to the army.
sentences.s552-s22: I possess goodness and a prosperous treasure; bravery, manliness, and a firm foundation exist.
sentences.s552-s23: We too are free of people's possessions, for people become enemies over a thing.
sentences.s552-s24: A path to us has been opened for you — by my kindness, among well‑wishing people.
